Class LoginState

java.lang.Object
View.Client.States.ConnectedState
View.Client.States.Connected.LoginState
All Implemented Interfaces:
ClientState
public final class LoginState extends ConnectedState
Represents the state of a client that is connected to the server and is in the process of logging in. This state allows the client to perform login actions and provides a list of available commands.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    LoginState(Network network)
    Constructs a LoginState with the specified network.

  • Constructor Details

    • LoginState

      public LoginState(Network network)
      Constructs a LoginState with the specified network.
      Parameters:
      network - The network associated with this client state.

  • Method Details

    • getAvailableCommands

      public List<String> getAvailableCommands()
      Returns a list of available commands in the login state. This includes the "Login" command and any commands available in the parent class.
      Returns:
      A list of strings representing available commands.

    • login

      public ClientState login(String username)
      Attempts to log in the user with the specified username. If the login is successful, it returns a ClientState representing the logged-in state. If the login is not confirmed, it returns an UnconfirmedLoginState.
      Parameters:
      username - The username of the user attempting to log in.
      Returns:
      A ClientState representing the result of the login attempt.
